summary.name: Kantakari
summary.scientific_name: Solanum virginianum L. (Syn. Solanum xanthocarpum Schrad. & Wendl.)

plant_profile.history: Unique History: Classified under the 'Dashamoola' (ten roots) and 'Kasaswashara' (respiratory-relieving) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Kantakari' literally translates to 'the one that has thorns' or 'the one that clears the throat'. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Physical Characteristics: A perennial, very prickly, prostrate herb with many branches. The leaves are ovate or elliptic, deeply lobed, with yellow-to-whitish sharp spines on both surfaces and along the midrib. The flowers are attractive blue-to-purple with yellow anthers. The fruit is a globose berry, yellowish-green (turning yellow when ripe) with white stripes. It is found as a wild weed in wastelands and sandy soils throughout India. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Steroidal Glycoalkaloids (Solasodine, Solamargine). The presence of solasodine is crucial for its corticosteroid-like anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects on the smooth muscles. Strict Standardizations: The whole plant is standardized in API based on its prickly nature and characteristic bitter-pungent taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPTLC quantification is used to monitor total alkaloid content calculated as solasodine.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Powerful Vata and Kapha Hara (pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas). Due to its Katu (pungent) and Tikta (bitter) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Kasaswashara' (respiratory-relieving) and 'Shothahara' (anti-inflammatory) therapies, clearing the cold, heavy, and sticky qualities of Kapha through its systemic warming and mucus-liquefying property. It may increase Pitta if used in excess.

medicinal_uses.expert_respiratory_and_cough_specialist: Kantakari is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of bronchial asthma and chronic cough. Its 'Tikshna' (sharp) and 'Ushna' (heating) nature provided by steroid alkaloids helps in liquefying and expelling tenacious mucus from the lungs. WHO-aligned research recognizes its potent bronchodilatory and anti-tussive effects.
medicinal_uses.asthma_and_bronchitis_management: Historically known as 'Shwasahara' (antiasthmatic). It effectively reduces the intensity and frequency of asthma attacks by clearing 'Kapha-avarana' (mucus-obstruction) in the respiratory channels and providing systemic warming (Ushna).
medicinal_uses.antipyretic_and_chronic_fever: Acts as a powerful agent for managing 'Jirna-jwara' (chronic/inflammatory fevers). It promotes diaphoresis and clears 'Ama' (toxins) responsible for systemic heat and body aches in long-standing infections.
medicinal_uses.urinary_and_kidney_care: As a mild Mutrala (diuretic), it helps in relieving urinary tract infections, burning urination (Mutra-kricchra), and localized edema (Shotha) by clearing Kapha-related obstructions in the bladder and kidneys.
medicinal_uses.digestive_fire_and_appetizer: Its Katu-Tikta (pungent-bitter) nature helps in igniting the 'Jatharagni' (digestive fire) and clearing stagnant toxins from the gut. It is traditionally used to manage 'Aruchi' (tastelessness) and flatulence.
medicinal_uses.anti_inflammatory_and_pain_relief: Systemically used for conditions like rheumatoid arthritis (Amavata) and chronic pleurisy (Parshvashoola) by clearing blocked Vata-Kapha pathways and reducing local swelling through its hot-sharp property.
medicinal_uses.phytochemical_profile: Rich in Steroidal Alkaloids (Solasodine, Solamargine, Solasurine), Glycoalkaloids, Steroids (Carpesterol), and Saponins. Solasodine is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory action.

preparation_methods.whole_plant_powder__churna_: 1 gram to 3 grams of fine powder taken twice daily with honey (for cough) or warm water (for general vitality).
preparation_methods.whole_plant_decoction__kwatha_: 20g of dried plant boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic asthma and bronchial congestion. Take twice daily before meals.
preparation_methods.kantakaryavaleha: Classical medicated jam taken (5-10g) twice daily for managing persistent cough and allergic asthma symptoms.
preparation_methods.kantakari_ghrita: 5-10g of ghee processed with Kantakari; taken to treat chronic respiratory Vata and physical weakness through its 'Brimhaniya' (nourishing) quality.
preparation_methods.external_paste: Ground fresh plant or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, joint pain) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.adults: Powder: 1-3 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Avaleha (Jam): 5-10 g, divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.children: Generally safe. 250 mg to 500 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for respiratory protection and cough relief.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata-Kapha respiratory issues. Dosage: 1g of powder daily with honey at bedtime.

contraindications: Generally safe. However, individuals with high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Avoid during pregnancy in very large doses except under expert guidance.
side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin warmth or stomach burning.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-asthmatic or diuretic medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and Siddha) without documented toxicity at therapeutic levels.
